subject: Re: Can I use M without having done the motioncorr in warp?
body:
Hi Carlos,

to get anything useful out of tilt series data, you'll have to put them 
through Warp I'm afraid. You can also start with motion-corrected averages 
(which you will also have to put through Warp first to get the metadata in 
place), but then you'll miss out on potentially better alignments. If you 
decide to process the movies with Warp, please note there will likely be a 
small offset in the particle positions compared to MotionCor2, so it's best 
to run a few iterations in RELION with the re-extracted particles 
afterwards.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Thursday, August 20, 2020 at 1:33:34 PM UTC+2, Carlos Fernández 
Rodríguez wrote:
>
>
> I have a dataset already processed in relion 3.1 and I just wanted to test 
> M for the polishing and the local filtering, but I dont have any .settings 
> file for the data source, or anything done in WARP.
>
> Can I still user M?
>
